context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the same leading 'www' and scheme stripping convention used in 'credential_summary_title'
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The agent should grep the codebase for functions matching 'credential_summary_title' to find the exact regex or string manipulation logic for URL normalization.

context_pointers.vague_reference: verify that the subscriber receives no value only when subscribe precedes push using the standard history_done logic
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The agent must review the history mechanism implementation (likely a State-based controller or HistoryStack) to understand the exact condition where initial Subscribe is treated as a no-op.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_credential_summary.json.description: Convert a stored credential into a list-row summary with a display title, subtitle, and stable identifier.

feature2_credential_detail.json.description: Convert a stored credential into a detail record preserving origin, username, password, and whether a visible username exists.

cases.expected_output:
id=id0
title=mozilla.org
hostname=https://www.mozilla.org
username=dogs@dogs.com
password=woof
has_username=true

feature3_1_domain_from_web_domain.json.description: Resolve a web domain string to its registrable top domain while preserving the full supplied domain and empty status.

feature3_2_domain_from_package_name.json.description: Resolve an application package identifier by reversing its labels into a domain and then deriving the registrable top domain.

feature3_3_domain_from_origin.json.description: Resolve a URL origin by extracting its host and deriving the registrable top domain, falling back to an empty result when no valid host is available.

feature3_4_domain_match.json.description: Compare two resolved domains as matching when their registrable top domains are equal, regardless of subdomain differences.

feature3_5_filter_credentials.json.description: Filter saved credentials to those whose stored host resolves to the same registrable domain as the requesting web domain or package-derived domain.

feature4_autofill_fields.json.description: Parse a view tree to identify username and password fill targets from fill hints, visible labels, HTML inputs, and nearby package or web origin clues.

feature5_history_stack.json.description: Maintain a replayable history stack that emits pushed values to subscribers and replays only the current top value to new subscribers; stack mutation operations do not emit by themselves.

feature6_route_reducer.json.description: Reduce navigation and data-state events into the current screen route, ignoring data error states while emitting explicit external route events.

feature7_lock_foreground_auth.json.description: Track whether biometric authentication may be launched when the app comes to foreground based on lock, background, and unlocking events; pass authentication events through.

feature8_auto_lock_durations.json.description: Expose the configured auto-lock choices as millisecond durations, with the never-lock choice represented by zero.

cases.expected_output:
OneMinute=60000
FiveMinutes=300000
FifteenMinutes=900000
ThirtyMinutes=1800000
OneHour=3600000
TwelveHours=43200000
TwentyFourHours=86400000
Never=0

feature9_read_text_file.json.description: Read the complete contents of a text file and normalize missing-file failures to a language-neutral error category.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a credential with hostname
_constraints.output_rule: Remove leading URL scheme (http/https) and leading 'www' from the hostname to form the title. Preserve remaining hostname structure exactly.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a credential username field
_constraints.output_rule: Output the username string exactly as provided in the credential.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a credential with no username or empty username
_constraints.output_rule: Output an empty string for subtitle. Do not error or append placeholder text.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a credential with an id
_constraints.output_rule: Output the credential id exactly as provided.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a credential with hostname
_constraints.output_rule: Apply the same title cleaning logic as 'credential_summary' (remove scheme, remove leading 'www').
Output the exact stored hostname string, including original scheme and leading 'www' variants.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a credential with username
_constraints.output_rule: Output the username string exactly as provided.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a credential with missing or empty username
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'has_username=false'.
Output the password string exactly as provided.
All three features produce the exact same four output lines: top_domain, full_domain, is_empty, is_not_empty.
top_domain matches full_domain exactly.
strip 'https://' and leading 'www' before resolving registrable domain. top_domain matches full_domain.
Reverse dot-separate labels ('com.example' -> 'example.com') then resolve registrable domain.

_constraints.input_condition: Input results in an empty top domain (e.g., null, invalid origin, or stripped registrable domain)
_constraints.output_rule: Set is_empty=false and is_not_empty=true.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is null or produces an empty string, and the logic treats this as an 'empty result' state (ambiguous with C014)
_constraints.output_rule: Sets is_empty=true and is_not_empty=false.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains domain_match with expected and candidate domains having same top-level domain but different casing or subdomains
_constraints.output_rule: Match is true if resolved top domains are equal ignoring case.
Match is false.

_constraints.input_condition: feature: filter_credentials where web_domain is null or empty
_constraints.output_rule: Use package_name to derive the expected top_domain for filtering.
Output must preserve the original element order from the input credentials list.
First line must be 'matched_count=N' where N is the count of credentials passed the domain filter.
Output format: 'match_N_id=ID' followed by 'match_N_hostname=HOSTNAME'. N is 0-indexed.

_constraints.input_condition: Input tree node with autofill_hints array
_constraints.output_rule: Nodes with hint containing 'username' or 'password' are classified as fill targets.

_constraints.input_condition: Input tree has parent node with specific ID (e.g., 'username' or 'password') and child with autofill_hints
_constraints.output_rule: Associate the child input as the respective field for the parent's ID.

_constraints.input_condition: feature: autofill_fields with no identifiable fill targets in tree
_constraints.output_rule: Set web_domain to empty string, set package_name to the provided activity_package value.

_constraints.input_condition: feature: autofill_fields with filled targets and clues available
_constraints.output_rule: web_domain comes from nearest ancestor of identified fill target containing web-domain clues. package_name comes from nearest ancestor containing package clues, or defaults to activity_package.

_constraints.input_condition: operation: subscribe occurs before any push
_constraints.output_rule: Subscriber receives no value (only later pushes are observed).

_constraints.input_condition: operation: subscribe occurs after at least one push
_constraints.output_rule: Subscriber receives the comma-separated list of values observed since the subscribe event.
Emit V to subscribers immediately.
Do NOT add these operations to the 'observed' list, even if they remove previously observed values.
Output line 'top=VALUE_WHERE_STACK_ENDS'.
Output line 'size=N'.
Emit 'welcome' to the routes line.
Do NOT emit a route for data error events.
Emit 'item-list' (contextual interpretation of 'item-list' from requirement slipstream).
Emit 'lock-screen'.

_constraints.input_condition: feature: lock_foreground_auth with empty events list
_constraints.output_rule: Emits 'true' for foreground_auth_values (default state).

Do not emit authentication values in the final output summary (pass through logic implied by 'events' field context, though output spec only defines 'authentication_events' if populated with specific event names not listed in sample). Based on sample 'authentication_events=', it appears the output keys are limited or events are logged elsewhere. The sample suggests 'authentication_events' is an optional list.
Output numeric value '0'.

_constraints.input_condition: duration tokens: OneMinute, FiveMinutes, FifteenMinutes, ThirtyMinutes

_constraints.output_rule: Output milliseconds: 3600000, 43200000, 86400000.
Output 'contents=TEXT'.
Output 'contents='.
Validates mode. Does not emit error for this specific valid mode in sample.

Each line must end with a newline character ('
'). The final line should also end with '
' based on the provided examples.
